title: Client-Side Attacks
body: Get a user to open/click something that runs your code. Pick the delivery based on the target: if Office may not be installed, the Windows Library + shortcut (.library-ms + .lnk) chain is the reliable pick — it needs no Office and shows no macro warning. HTA and Office macros are alternatives. Set up your listener BEFORE sending anything.
cmd:
# Decide delivery:
#   .library-ms + WebDAV + .lnk -> no Office needed, no macro prompt (PEN-200 go-to)
#   HTA                        -> single file, runs via mshta.exe
#   Office macro               -> needs Office + macros enabled (.doc, not .docx)
#   macro_pack                 -> generator: spits out .hta/.lnk/.url/Office from one tool
warn: Listener first, payload second. If you send the phish before your WebDAV / web server / nc listener are all up, the one click you get is wasted.

choices.label: Library file + WebDAV + shortcut (no Office, no macro warning)
